qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded EXR image dimensions trigger allocation-size-too-big aborts in bundled CLI tools
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a local oversized-header allocation DoS in exr2aces/exrmakepreview, while the advisory root cause is truncated PXR24 decompression causing uninitialized-heap disclosure in undo_pxr24_impl after LIBDEFLATE_SHORT_OUTPUT is treated as success.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described PXR24 decompression root cause or the affected code in src/lib/OpenEXRCore/internal_pxr24.c and src/lib/OpenEXRCore/compression.c.
evidence: The session manifest lists exactly one real qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_8fe894f5/unbounded_image_dimensions_cli_dos.
That finding's report and reproduction target oversized EXR header dimensions accepted by exrheader, followed by allocation-size-too-big aborts in exr2aces/exrmakepreview.

The advisory and its fix target a different mechanism: truncated zlib output accepted by exr_uncompress_buffer and over-read of scratch data in undo_pxr24_impl during PXR24 decompression, yielding heap information disclosure rather than header-size allocation failure.
